notes: Library card sections completed for parent choice: reading, vocabulary, journals, writing, questions, informational text, and assessments.
Structured from existing markdown unit card.

book.title: Alice's Adventures in Wonderland
book.author: Lewis Carroll
book.description: Author: Lewis Carroll Grade Level: 4th Grade (Ages 9-10) Unit Length: 30 lessons (24 regular + 6 assessments) Days: 121-150 (Weeks 25-30) Theme: Imagination, Nonsense, Logic vs. Absurdity, Growing Up Reading Level: Classic fantasy This card now includes complete weekly library sections for Grade 4 use.

weeks.title: Week 25: Down the Rabbit Hole (Days 121-125)
reading: Down the Rabbit Hole, Pool of Tears, Caucus Race
journal: What does this week's reading show about Imagination? Use one scene as evidence.
Choose one character decision from Down the Rabbit Hole, Pool of Tears, Caucus Race. Would you make the same choice? Explain why.
writing: Write an ACE paragraph answering one comprehension question with text evidence.
Draft a short narrative response connected to this week's reading.
questions: What problem or conflict matters most in Down the Rabbit Hole, Pool of Tears, Caucus Race?
Which detail best reveals a character trait, motivation, or change?
How does the setting affect what happens in this section?
What lesson, theme, or big idea is starting to develop?
assessment: Weekly assessment or review day included in source card.
